purpose: Primary rotational drive for the machine spindle. Converts electrical voltage commands into mechanical torque and speed, while managing thermal dynamics and velocity control accuracy. Degradation of motor windings, insulation, and control fidelity directly impacts machining precision and reliability.

inputs.name: motor_voltage_command
inputs.description: Commanded voltage level sent to the spindle motor drive, expressed as a percentage of maximum drive voltage. Typical range 30–60%.

outputs.name: actual_spindle_speed
outputs.description: Measured rotational speed of the spindle motor output shaft. Range 0 to 32767 rpm. Reduced by velocity control degradation.

outputs.name: zero_speed_detection
outputs.description: True when the spindle speed is at or below the zero-speed threshold, indicating the motor has stopped or nearly stopped.

outputs.name: motor_current
outputs.description: Measured RMS current drawn by the motor. Increases with load and degrades with winding/insulation degradation. Range 0–100 A.

outputs.name: drive_torque
outputs.description: Mechanical torque delivered at the spindle output. Derived from motor current and torque constant, reduced by position accuracy degradation.

states.name: motor_temperature
states.description: Thermal accumulation state representing motor winding temperature. Rises with electrical load (proportional to voltage command squared) and decays toward ambient via convective cooling. Clamped between ambient (25 °C) and physical destruction limit (200 °C).

states.name: velocity_error_accumulation
states.description: Accumulated magnitude of velocity control error. Builds when the motor cannot track the commanded speed (e.g., due to load disturbances or control degradation) and decays when tracking is restored. Clamped to [0, 5000] rpm.

states.name: position_accuracy_degradation
states.degradation_timescale: ~33 operating cycles (derived from bearing_wear_rate proxy; no direct simulator rate provided — see assumptions)
states.description: Normalised health index for encoder/coder signal fidelity and positional accuracy. Starts at 1.0 (perfect) and degrades toward 0.0 as encoder wear, vibration, and thermal cycling accumulate. Drives position error growth in output logic.

states.name: commanded_speed_tracking
states.description: First-order lag estimate of the speed the motor is attempting to reach, derived from the voltage command. Used internally to compute velocity error accumulation without referencing inputs directly in event thresholds.

events.name: motor_overheat
events.description: Motor overheat alarm. Triggered when motor_temperature exceeds the overheat_threshold (140 °C). Under healthy initial conditions motor_temperature starts at 25 °C, so event_state = 1.0 at initialisation.

events.name: excessive_velocity_error
events.description: Excessive velocity error alarm. Triggered when velocity_error_accumulation exceeds the velocity_error_threshold parameter. Under healthy initial conditions velocity_error_accumulation = 0, so event_state = 1.0 at initialisation.

events.name: overspeed
events.description: Overspeed alarm. Triggered when commanded_speed_tracking exceeds the overspeed_threshold parameter, indicating the motor is being driven beyond its rated maximum speed. Healthy initial state = 0 rpm, so event_state = 1.0 at initialisation.

events.name: short_time_overload
events.description: Short-time overload alarm. Triggered when motor_temperature exceeds the short_time_overload_temp_threshold AND velocity_error_accumulation exceeds the short_time_overload_error_threshold simultaneously, indicating a transient overload condition. Both states start within safe bounds, so event_state = 1.0 at initialisation.

events.name: motor_lock
events.description: Motor lock alarm. Triggered when velocity_error_accumulation is high and position_accuracy_degradation has fallen below the motor_lock_accuracy_threshold, indicating the rotor has stalled or the encoder has lost tracking. Healthy initial states keep event_state = 1.0.

events.name: current_overload
events.description: Current overload alarm. Triggered when motor_temperature exceeds the critical_temperature (150 °C) or position_accuracy_degradation falls below the current_overload_accuracy_threshold, reflecting winding degradation causing excessive current draw. Healthy initial states keep event_state = 1.0.

parameters.name: motor_heating_rate
parameters.description: Rate of temperature rise per unit of normalised voltage command squared per time step. Represents resistive (I²R) heating scaled to the voltage command.

parameters.name: motor_cooling_rate
parameters.description: Convective cooling rate constant. Temperature decays toward ambient at this rate per time step when load is reduced.

parameters.name: ambient_temperature
parameters.description: Ambient reference temperature toward which the motor cools.

parameters.name: overheat_threshold
parameters.description: Motor temperature at which the motor_overheat alarm is triggered. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_temperature
parameters.description: Motor temperature at which current_overload failure is declared. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: velocity_error_buildup_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which velocity error accumulates per unit of normalised voltage command when the motor is under load stress.

parameters.name: velocity_error_decay_rate
parameters.description: Rate at which accumulated velocity error decays per time step when the motor is tracking normally.

parameters.name: velocity_error_threshold
parameters.description: Accumulated velocity error above which the excessive_velocity_error alarm is triggered.

parameters.name: position_accuracy_degradation_rate
parameters.description: Rate of position accuracy degradation per time step driven by load_cycles_and_speed. Derived from bearing_wear_rate proxy (0.02) since no direct simulator rate is provided for this state.

parameters.name: speed_tracking_lag_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for commanded_speed_tracking state. Capped at 0.5 for numerical stability.

parameters.name: max_commanded_speed
parameters.description: Maximum rated spindle speed corresponding to 100% voltage command.

parameters.name: overspeed_threshold
parameters.description: Speed above which the overspeed alarm is triggered.

parameters.name: zero_speed_threshold
parameters.description: Speed below which zero_speed_detection is set to true. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: motor_torque_constant
parameters.description: Proportionality constant between motor current and output torque.

parameters.name: motor_current_gain
parameters.description: Proportionality constant mapping normalised voltage command to motor current under no-load conditions.

parameters.name: current_overload_accuracy_threshold
parameters.description: Position accuracy degradation health index below which current_overload is declared due to winding degradation.

parameters.name: motor_lock_accuracy_threshold
parameters.description: Position accuracy degradation health index below which motor_lock is declared when combined with high velocity error.

parameters.name: motor_lock_error_threshold
parameters.description: Velocity error accumulation above which motor_lock is declared when combined with low position accuracy.

parameters.name: short_time_overload_temp_threshold
parameters.description: Motor temperature threshold for the short_time_overload alarm.

parameters.name: short_time_overload_error_threshold
parameters.description: Velocity error accumulation threshold for the short_time_overload alarm.

parameters.name: speed_arrival_threshold
parameters.description: Speed difference below which the motor is considered to have arrived at commanded speed. From simulator ground truth.

parameters.name: critical_position_error
parameters.description: Critical position error limit from simulator ground truth, used to scale position accuracy degradation into physical position error for output logic.

state_transition_logic: speed_tracking_lag_coefficient is capped at 0.5 for numerical stability
driver = load_cycles_and_speed (proxy: motor_voltage_command / 100.0, representing combined load and speed stress)
output_logic: Base speed from voltage command via first-order lag: commanded_speed_tracking
Degradation reduces tracking fidelity: actual_spindle_speed = commanded_speed_tracking * position_accuracy_degradation
Returns true (1) when actual_spindle_speed <= 0.75 rpm, false (0) otherwise
Base current proportional to voltage command: base_current = motor_current_gain * motor_voltage_command
Thermal derating: as motor_temperature approaches critical_temperature, effective resistance rises, increasing current
Torque derived from current via torque constant, reduced by position accuracy degradation (encoder/control fidelity loss)
event_logic: Fault triggers when event_state <= 0.0 (velocity_error_accumulation >= velocity_error_threshold = 500.0 rpm)
Fault triggers when event_state <= 0.0 (commanded_speed_tracking >= overspeed_threshold = 32767.0 rpm)
Fault triggers when both margins <= 0.0 simultaneously
Fault triggers when velocity_error_accumulation >= motor_lock_error_threshold AND position_accuracy_degradation <= motor_lock_accuracy_threshold
Fault triggers when motor_temperature >= critical_temperature (150 °C) OR position_accuracy_degradation <= current_overload_accuracy_threshold
assumptions: position_accuracy_degradation uses position_accuracy_degradation_rate = 0.02 (borrowed from bearing_wear_rate proxy) because the simulator ground truth provides no dedicated rate for encoder/coder degradation. This should be replaced with a validated rate when available.

The simulator ground truth does not list a direct degradation state for motor_temperature or velocity_error_accumulation; these are modelled as accumulation states with physically motivated heating/cooling and buildup/decay rates that are fully tunable parameters.

motor_voltage_command (%) is used as a proxy for the load_cycles_and_speed driver for position_accuracy_degradation, since the actual load-cycle count is not an available input port. This is a conservative approximation.

actual_spindle_speed is estimated internally as commanded_speed_tracking * position_accuracy_degradation for the purpose of computing velocity error; the true measured speed is the output, not an input, so this internal estimate avoids circular dependency.

The short_time_overload event uses a conjunctive (AND) condition on temperature and velocity error, reflecting that a transient overload requires both elevated thermal state and control stress simultaneously.
motor_lock uses a conjunctive condition on velocity_error_accumulation and position_accuracy_degradation, reflecting that a true lock requires both high error and degraded encoder fidelity.
current_overload uses a disjunctive (min) condition: either critical temperature OR severely degraded position accuracy can independently cause excessive current draw.
The speed_tracking_lag_coefficient default of 0.3 is below the stability cap of 0.5 and represents a moderately responsive motor drive. It is a tunable parameter.
ambient_temperature is fixed at 25.0 °C. In a more detailed model this could be an input port representing enclosure temperature.
overspeed_threshold is set equal to max_commanded_speed (32767 rpm) as a conservative default; in practice the drive may have a separate hardware overspeed limit slightly above the rated maximum.
zero_speed_threshold value of 0.75 rpm is taken directly from the simulator ground truth.
speed_arrival_threshold value of 15.0 rpm is taken directly from the simulator ground truth and used to gate velocity error accumulation vs. decay.

critical_position_error of 2000.0 rpm (as listed in simulator ground truth) is retained as a parameter for output scaling even though its unit appears to be rpm rather than a positional unit — this may reflect a velocity-domain position error metric in the simulator.
